text: Day 2 had bright sunshine from 8am to 11am, but they started the game at 11am. We were able to get only 33 overs that day. I'm not asking them to start at 8, but at least be flexible and start one hour early, especially when you know rain is around the corner.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: In addition to travel cost there's the early-morning dew. The old 60-over domestic games started at 10.30am and it was a huge advantage to bowl first and nip a few wickets out with the prodigious swing. But once you lose more than a session to rain we should extend play by 30 minutes both before and after. Bad light is the really annoying one — the red ball is tough to pick under lights compared to a white ball. Much stricter penalties on over rates would go a long way.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: Adding roofs to existing stadiums isn't how construction works. The entire stadium would need to be reconfigured from the ground up, to the point you may as well just build new stadiums with retractable roofs included. The price tags are astronomical and a retractable roof can cost anywhere from $25 to $50 million, not including the stadium itself.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: Their footprint is still only around half of a large cricket ground. The playing area at the Rogers Centre in Toronto is roughly a square with 100m sides, so call it 12,000 m2. The playing area at Lord's is almost 22,000 m2. Doubling the size of something does more than just double how complicated it is.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: There are baseball stadiums in Toronto, Seattle, Milwaukee and Houston that have retractable roofs, so engineering-wise it is definitely doable as the footprint is not too dissimilar to a cricket ground. The prohibiting factor is the cost and the fact these need to be built from the ground up. The ECB just doesn't have the money to justify this.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: UK summers have long days. Sun rises before 6am and sets after 9pm. Why start test matches at 11? Start at 9.30 and end the day by 4-5. That gives you time for extended play as well.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: It's too dewy to start at 9:30am and would kill attendances. In the early 2000s test matches in England started at 10:30am because of broadcast scheduling, and it was pretty unpopular because of the cost of travelling before 10am.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: There needs to be provision for playing sides to alter the rules. Before the toss, the teams should be allowed to unanimously increase playing time. For the last match they could have agreed to play 1.5 hours extra per day, 30 minutes before and an hour after. Worst case, if no rain comes you get an extra 75 overs — better than a washout.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: The broadcast deal is for a set amount with revenue divided amongst the counties; ticket revenue goes to the counties to cover hosting costs. So if attendances drop it's the counties who shoulder the burden, and in the past low attendances have left some counties in serious trouble — Yorkshire famously lost a fortune hosting a neutral Pakistan vs Australia test in 2010.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)
label: analysis

text: This washout is getting more attention than it deserves, and only because India was in a stronger position going into day 5. Had England been strong, not many would've cared. We've had washouts before and will again. A reserve day could mean fewer tests per series, which we won't like.
thread_title: India vs England Test rain washout discussion (Trent Bridge 2021)

text: Get rid of the tea break. If 50 overs can be played in one stretch in an ODI with only a 5-minute drinks break, why does a slower format need a tea break? That 45 minutes could be used for actual playing. Also cover the whole outfield like they do in Sri Lanka — most grounds have good drainage yet delays happen due to a wet outfield.
